title: SOS Care Text
text:
SOS Care Text - SupportGoWhere
SOS Care Text
Share link
24/7 confidential text support for suicide prevention and crisis intervention, accessible to all but targeting at youths from the ages 10 to 29 years old with suicide risk.
SERVICE HIGHLIGHTS
Helpline
Free
Referral not required
HELPLINE
9151 1767
SERVICE PROVIDERS
Find service providers near you
Collapse all sections
About this support

Samaritans of Singapore (SOS) Care Text is dedicated to providing confidential emotional support to individuals facing a crisis, thinking about suicide or affected by suicide. This crisis intervention and suicide prevention work is done through text messaging.

This programme contributes to the identification of individuals who are suicidal or in crisis, initiation of early intervention and preventive measures, and connecting these individuals to appropriate resources where necessary

This programme is targeted at youths from the ages of 10 to 29 years old with suicide risk, but not limited to this group and anyone can access this service. Programme aims to reduce suicide risks for persons in distress through texting which is a preference for the youths. The SOS Care Text is available round-the-clock to anyone who requires emotional support and or is affected by suicide and complements the current SOS crisis hotline service. The text service is anonymous.

Who is this for?

This programme is targeted at youths from the ages of 10 to 29 years old with suicide risk, but not limited to this group and anyone can access this service. Programme aims to reduce suicide risks for persons in distress through texting which is a preference for the youths. The SOS Care Text is available round-the-clock to anyone who requires emotional support and or is affected by suicide and complements the current SOS crisis hotline service. The text service is anonymous.

What to expect?

Clients can access care text by texting in to WhatsApp 9151 1767. 24-hour text messaging platform manned by trained volunteers and supported by professional staff.

Depending on need, clients may be encouraged to seek immediate support from the hospitals, psychiatrists, therapists, counsellors and community services or encouraged to see a counsellor at SOS.

How to apply?

Client access care text by texting in to WhatsApp 9151 1767.

Information last updated on 22 Oct 2025
